message:
style: rustfmt normalization pass across ABI and ABI tests

Pure `cargo fmt` cleanup across frankenlibc-abi crates — no behaviour
change. The tree had drifted from rustfmt's preferred output in a
handful of files and this commit brings them back so `cargo fmt
--check` and CI stay green without an ad-hoc fix-up step.

Normalized items:

- **Printf-family short if/else.** In `stdio_abi.rs` the tail of
  `fprintf`/`printf`/`dprintf`/`vfprintf`/`vprintf`/`vdprintf` had a
  single-line `if adverse { -1 } else { printf_result_to_c_int(...) }`.
  The block crosses rustfmt's line-length threshold at that call site
  (the `runtime_policy::scaled_cost(15, total_len)` preamble eats the
  budget), so rustfmt wraps it onto three lines. Done in all six
  printf-style wrappers for consistency.
- **Let-chain in printf %n guard.** Same file: the `%n` bounds check
  in `render_printf` was nested as
  `if let Some(rem) = known_remaining(ptr_val) { if rem < size { ... } }`.
  Rewritten using the Rust 2024 let-chain form
  `if let Some(rem) = known_remaining(ptr_val) && rem < size { ... }`,
  which is what rustfmt now emits for this shape. Semantically
  identical — preserves the bd-xx2j1 guarantee that untracked
  allocations do NOT suppress %n writes (the explicit "No else
  branch" comment is kept).
- **`scandirat` malloc cast wrap.** `glibc_internal_abi.rs` had the
  `libc::malloc(...) as *mut *mut c_void` spread across three lines
  with an extra indent; rustfmt now folds the first two into a single
  unsafe block with the cast trailing on the next line.
- **`__cxa_thread_atexit_impl` dlsym wrap.** `startup_abi.rs` — same
  shape as above; the dlsym call is now a single-line unsafe block
  with the trailing `as usize` cast on the next line, indent level
  aligned with the `let resolved =` binding rather than leading
  inside the closure.
- **`xdr_pmaplist` more-flag.** `rpc_abi.rs` had a single-line
  `if ... { 1 } else { 0 }` which was over the 100-col limit with the
  leading `let mut more: c_int =`; expanded to the 5-line form.
- **Trailing blank line.** `pthread_abi.rs` removed a stray trailing
  blank line inside the `mod tests` block.
- **`format!` collapse.** `stdlib_abi.rs` had a multi-line
  `format!("{f}: {line}: {func}: Unexpected error: {msg}.\n")` that
  fits on one line under rustfmt's width heuristic; collapsed.
  Separately, the `scan_c_string` `let` binding lost a trailing
  indent level to match.
- **Test args wrapping.** `io_internal_native_file_test.rs`,
  `pthread_abi_test.rs`, `xdr_abi_test.rs` reflowed multi-arg calls
  (`_IO_adjust_column`, `_IO_adjust_wcolumn`, `pthread_atfork`,
  `xdr_pmaplist`) and struct literals (`timespec { tv_sec: 0,
  tv_nsec }`) to match rustfmt's preferred one-arg-per-line /
  single-line shapes.

Verified with `cargo fmt --check` before/after — the diff is exactly
what rustfmt wants and nothing else. No public API, signature, or
behavioural change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
